location.office_time_required: part time telecommuting is an option; hybrid work from SoFi office in New York, NY

level_signals: 3 years of experience in the job offered or related occupation (post-Master's)
Comp range $214,000–$235,400 base, consistent with Senior IC at a large fintech

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Master's degree in Business Administration, Information Technology or related quantitative discipline and three (3) years of experience in the job offered or related occupation.
three (3) years of experience in the job offered or related occupation.
Experience defining product requirements, user stories, and functional specifications for digital consumer products.
Experience analyzing product performance metrics, user behavior data, and conversion funnel data to inform product decisions.
Experience designing, executing, and evaluating controlled product experiments to assess feature performance and improve conversion or engagement.
Experience improving customer onboarding, application flows, or promotions/benefits experiences for digital products.
Experience working with engineering, design, marketing, and operations teams to plan and deliver product initiatives.
Experience participating in Agile or iterative product development processes, including backlog prioritization and release planning.
Experience preparing and presenting product plans, analysis, and results to technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Experience collaborating with legal, risk, or compliance teams to ensure product features align with applicable policies or regulatory requirements.
Experience supporting product-led growth initiatives by leveraging product features, user journeys, and experimentation to drive acquisition, activation, or engagement.
Experience working on digital products within the financial services or financial technology industry.

explicitly_excluded: Remote work from Hawaii or Alaska (due to insurance coverage issues)

scope.surface: SoFi Money — consumer banking digital product including customer onboarding, application flow, and benefits/promotions experiences
scope.business_model: B2C consumer banking / fintech, national bank
core_responsibilities: Plan, develop, and optimize digital product features related to customer onboarding, application flow, and benefits/promotions experiences for consumer banking products
Define product requirements and success metrics, analyzing conversion and engagement data
Design and execute experiments to improve application start, onboarding completion, and product adoption
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to implement product enhancements, ensure regulatory alignment, and support growth and customer engagement objectives

green_flags: Hybrid policy allows part-time telecommuting — not fully on-site
Clear, specific skill requirements enumerated (10 special skill requirements)
Role has well-defined product surface (SoFi Money, onboarding and growth)
red_flags: Master's degree listed as a hard requirement, which is relatively rare and restrictive for a PM role (though 'any suitable combination' clause softens this)
No equity mentioned — total compensation picture is incomplete

JD reads like a formal Department of Labor posting (likely for PERM/immigration purposes), which may indicate this role is tied to or being run in parallel with a visa sponsorship process — worth confirming with recruiter
No nice-to-haves listed, making the requirements appear binary and inflexible

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from title, comp, and years requirement)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not directly listed in JD)
ambiguities: The JD format (enumerated special skill requirements, Master's as baseline, 'any suitable combination' clause, ATTN: HR submission instructions, Req.# format) is consistent with a PERM labor certification posting for immigration purposes. This may mean the role is specifically open to or being filed for a visa-sponsored candidate, but this is not confirmed.

The 'any suitable combination of education, training and/or experience is acceptable' clause potentially softens the Master's degree hard requirement, but the primary statement lists it as mandatory. Treated as hard with a note on substitutability.
No nice-to-haves are stated anywhere in the JD — all listed requirements appear as mandatory special skills.
